preset_description: Dark = substance signal. Spotlights product capabilities and metrics. CTA and pricing are understated — features are the story, not conversion pressure.

brief.audience: Founding teams who want an AI-first design system

accent_tokens._note: Brand primary CTA color. Global — not per-preset. Two variants: button on a light/neutral section vs. on a dark section.

beats.name: Orientation
beats.rationale: Establishes context for founding teams seeking an AI-first design system. Anchors the page with a clear statement of who MODE is for and what it delivers, giving the Validator archetype immediate clarity before any persuasion begins.

beats.name: Credibility
beats.rationale: Validator archetypes need trust signals early. Presenting customer logos, testimonials from other founding teams, or notable backers addresses their instinct to verify legitimacy before engaging deeper with evidence or pricing.

beats.name: Evidence
beats.rationale: Required by both the decision funnel stage and the Validator archetype. Concrete metrics—adoption rates, time-to-ship improvements, performance benchmarks—give the proof-driven Validator the quantitative confidence they need to move toward a purchase decision.

beats.name: Decision
beats.rationale: Required at the decision funnel stage. Presents pricing tiers and plan comparisons in a clear, framed choice structure. Positioned after Evidence so the Validator has already internalized the proof that justifies the investment.

beats.name: Conversion
beats.rationale: Immediately precedes Recovery per structural rules. Delivers the final CTA—start a paid plan or begin a trial—capitalizing on the credibility and evidence momentum built above. Direct, high-commitment prompt aligned with the convert-to-paid goal.

beats.name: Recovery
beats.rationale: Always last. Provides secondary paths—contact sales, explore docs, revisit case studies—so Validators who aren't fully convinced yet have a way to continue researching rather than bouncing entirely.

sections.name: Navigation
sections.rationale: Global navigation — wayfinding and persistent CTA for the session.

sections.name: Hero

sections.rationale: Required by both the decision funnel stage and the Validator archetype. Concrete metrics—adoption rates, time-to-ship improvements, performance benchmarks—give the proof-driven Validator the quantitative confidence they need to move toward a purchase decision.

sections.name: Pricing
sections.rationale: Required at the decision funnel stage. Presents pricing tiers and plan comparisons in a clear, framed choice structure. Positioned after Evidence so the Validator has already internalized the proof that justifies the investment.

sections.name: CTA
sections.rationale: Immediately precedes Recovery per structural rules. Delivers the final CTA—start a paid plan or begin a trial—capitalizing on the credibility and evidence momentum built above. Direct, high-commitment prompt aligned with the convert-to-paid goal.

sections.name: Footer
sections.rationale: Page close — brand mark, minimal navigation, and legal.

page.reasoning: Decision funnel stage requires conversion focus. Notes specify: 'minimal variant for conversion-focused pages — strip nav_links, keep logo and single CTA only.'

Decision funnel stage prioritizes conversion over exploration. Notes state: 'For conversion stage use text-only: slim title and subhead only, no media, no secondary CTA — orients the visitor without competing with the decision moment below.'

slots.eyebrow: AI-first design systems for founding teams
slots.headline: Your design system knows how to render. MODE teaches it why.
slots.subhead: The semantic intelligence layer that resolves component behavior, visual emphasis, and copy register to the right experience — by archetype, funnel stage, and context. No variants. No manual QA.
slots.trust_signal: One-time payment. Lifetime access. No subscription.

page.reasoning: Validator archetype requires credibility signals at decision stage. Notes specify: 'Always include role and company for Validator credibility' and 'with-company-logo for enterprise social proof. Place near a CTA — testimonials convert when positioned at a decision moment.'

slots.quote: We shipped three audience-aware landing page variants in the time it used to take us to finish one. The resolution engine makes decisions we were manually configuring before — and it makes them correctly.

page.reasoning: Validator archetype demands attribution for trust. Notes state: 'Always pair stats with source attribution in Validator contexts — unsourced stats reduce trust' and '3-stat is the preferred default.'

slots.headline: What founding teams report after shipping with MODE

stats.label: reduction in manual variant configuration time
median time from install to first agent-generated page architecture
more audience variants shipped per sprint without additional design cycles

page.reasoning: Conversion stage requires clear recommendation signal. Notes indicate: 'Validator variant includes description and longer feature list' and 'highlighted variant for recommended plan — use sparingly (one per pricing section).'

slots.description: The full intelligence layer. Resolution engine, component manifest, token config, palette map, IA planner, multi-variant generation, and Next.js preview UI. Yours forever.
features: Three palette presets — funnel, archetype, feature-emphasis
Bring your own Anthropic API key
Lifetime access — no subscription, no lock-in

page.reasoning: Validator archetype at decision stage needs trust signals. Notes specify: 'Validator variant: include subhead and trust_signal (e.g., 'No credit card required').'

slots.headline: The intelligence layer is ready. So is the decision.
slots.subhead: MODE ships as a code kit — running in under an hour, owned by your team, no runtime dependency. One payment. No renewal.
slots.trust_signal: One-time payment. Lifetime access. Fork it, extend it, make it yours.

page.reasoning: Decision funnel stage requires conversion recovery. Notes state: 'with-cta variant recaptures departing users on key conversion pages — use on pricing and signup flows.'
